zones.label: UART
zones.summary: Universal Async Receiver/Transmitter — the oldest, simplest serial link
facts: Wires: VCC, GND, TX (transmit), RX (receive)
Asynchronous serial — no clock line; each device keeps its own clock
Full-duplex: TX and RX are independent so both run at once
Start/stop bits frame each byte; configurable baud rate (9,600 to 1,000,000+ bps)
Point-to-point only — one transmitter, one receiver
Connectors: JST-PH (4-pin), Dupont/Header (4-pin)
Use cases: serial console/debugging, Bluetooth modules, WiFi modules, GPS, modems
Simple, reliable, and supported by every microcontroller

zones.label: 1-Wire
zones.summary: One data line for temperature sensors and addressable LEDs
facts: Wires: VCC, GND, and a single DQ (1-Wire) data bus
Uses a single data line for communication — very few pins
Some devices (e.g. DS18B20) can be parasitically powered with no VCC, using the data line + pull-up resistor
Many devices can share the same data line
Connectors: JST-PH (3-pin), Dupont/Header (3-pin)
Temperature sensors: DS18B20, DS18S20, DS1822, waterproof probes
Addressable LEDs: NeoPixel/WS2812/SK6812 strips, rings, matrices
Ideal for sensors and LEDs that share one data line

zones.label: I2S
zones.summary: Inter-IC Sound — a dedicated digital audio bus
facts: Wires: VCC, GND, SCK/BCLK (bit clock), WS/LRCLK (word select), SD/DOUT, SD/DIN
Synchronous serial protocol designed specifically for audio data
Uses a bit clock (BCLK) and a left/right word-select clock (LRCLK)
Typically full-duplex; supports high sample rates and bit depths
Common sample rates: 8 kHz – 192 kHz+; bit depths 16, 24, 32 bits
Not for control or commands — audio data only
Connectors: JST-PH (6-pin), Dupont/Header (6-pin)
Use cases: audio DACs and ADCs, amplifiers, digital microphones, audio streaming

quiz.question: Which protocol is a dedicated digital audio bus that is not used for general control or commands?
quiz.explanation: I2S (Inter-IC Sound) carries only audio data, using a bit clock and a left/right word-select clock. It is purpose-built for DACs, ADCs, amplifiers, and digital microphones.

quiz.question: Which protocol uses a single data line and powers temperature sensors like the DS18B20?
quiz.explanation: 1-Wire uses one DQ data line shared by many devices. Sensors like the DS18B20 can even be parasitically powered from that line plus a pull-up resistor — no separate VCC needed.

quiz.question: Which protocol is point-to-point, has no clock line, and is used for the serial debug console?
quiz.explanation: UART is asynchronous (no shared clock — each side keeps its own and they agree on a baud rate) and connects exactly two devices. It is the standard for serial console/REPL debugging.

quiz.question: Which protocol drives addressable LEDs like NeoPixel/WS2812 strips?
quiz.explanation: On this poster, addressable LEDs (NeoPixel/WS2812/SK6812) are grouped under the single-data-line '1-Wire' family — many LEDs share one data line, just like 1-Wire sensors.

quiz.question: Which protocol needs a word-select (left/right) clock to separate stereo audio channels?
quiz.explanation: I2S uses the WS/LRCLK (word-select) line to tell the receiver whether each sample is the left or right channel, alongside the BCLK bit clock — essential for stereo audio.
